dominants.dominant_desc:
Lunox is strongest in the mid to late game when she has completed her core items. During the early game, focus on safely clearing minion waves and farming to reach level 4 as quickly as possible. Avoid overextending and use your Order skills to sustain and stay safe in lane. Always watch the map and rotate to help teammates when possible.
In the mid game, Lunox becomes more powerful and can start joining team fights. Use your Chaos skills to deal high burst damage to enemy backline heroes like marksmen and mages. Position yourself behind tanks and avoid getting caught by enemy assassins. Use Flicker or Order ultimate to escape dangerous situations.
In the late game, Lunox becomes extremely dangerous due to her high magic penetration and burst damage. She can melt both tanks and squishy heroes quickly. Focus on proper positioning and target priority. Always attack from a safe distance and wait for the right moment to enter fights. With proper timing and positioning, Lunox can carry team fights and secure victory

emblems.emblem_desc: I chose the Assassin emblem set for Lunox because it provides extra magic penetration, adaptive damage, and mobility, which increases her burst damage and overall effectiveness. Lunox relies on dealing high magic damage quickly, and the Assassin emblem helps her eliminate enemies faster, especially squishy targets

equips.equip_desc: I chose this build to maximize Lunox magic penetration, burst damage, and scaling, allowing her to deal high damage to both squishy and tank heroes throughout the game

hero.hero_overview: Lunox is a powerful mage hero in Mobile Legends who specializes in dealing high burst magic damage while balancing her Order and Chaos powers. She is unique because she relies on her duality system, allowing her to switch between burst damage and sustained damage modes. Lunox excels in the mid lane where she can farm safely and quickly rotate to assist teammates. Her Chaos abilities deal massive damage to enemies, especially tanks, while her Order abilities provide sustain and survivability. Lunox is very effective in team fights, objective control, and eliminating high-priority targets like marksmen and mages.
hero.hero_strength:
High burst magic damage, especially in Chaos state
Strong against tank heroes due to percentage-based damage
Has invincibility and sustain from Order abilities
Strong in mid and late game team fights
Can easily eliminate squishy enemies
Good mobility and survivability when played properly
hero.hero_weakness:
Requires proper positioning and timing
 No strong crowd control skills
Vulnerable when skills are on cooldown
Easily killed if caught by assassins
Requires practice to master her Order and Chaos mechanics 
Relies heavily on skill accuracy

data.recommend: This guide is recommended for players who want to master Lunox and improve their performance in Mobile Legends, especially in the mid lane role

spell.spell_desc: Flicker is the best battle spell for Lunox because it provides instant repositioning, allowing her to escape dangerous situations or chase enemies. Since Lunox has no dash skill, Flicker helps improve her mobility and survivability. It can be used to avoid enemy crowd control, escape assassins, or reposition safely during team fights. Flicker also allows Lunox to surprise enemies by blinking forward and quickly using her Chaos skills to deal burst damage. This spell makes Lunox safer and more effective, especially in high-risk team fights.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Hero dengan Ultimate yang mampu mengumpulkan musuh di satu titik adalah pasangan terbaik Lunox. Saat musuh berkumpul, ledakan AOE dari kombo Flicker + Ultimate Chaos kamu akan mengenai semua target sekaligus, memberikan instant wipe-out.

Lunox sangat rentan saat melakukan spam skill ungu di tengah kerumunan musuh. Shield dan tambahan Movement Speed dari Angela memberikan perlindungan (sustain) ekstra, membuatmu bisa bertahan lebih lama saat mengeksekusi lini belakang.
Skill ini menghubungkan beberapa hero musuh sekaligus. Semua damage dan efek crowd control yang diterima oleh satu hero akan disalurkan ke hero lainnya yang terhubung.

counters.counter_desc: Efek Suppress adalah satu-satunya hal yang tidak bisa dihindari oleh mode Light Lunox. Jika mereka menangkapmu sebelum kamu sempat menekan Ultimate, kombo agresifmu akan terhenti seketika.
Silence dari Helcurt mencegahmu berganti stack, sementara burst cepat dari Saber bisa melenyapkanmu sebelum kamu sempat masuk ke mode invincible (kuning).
Mereka memiliki jarak serang yang jauh lebih luas. Jika kamu tidak memiliki Flicker untuk mendekat, mereka bisa mencicil HP-mu dari jarak aman sebelum kamu sempat menjangkau mereka.

dominants.dominant_desc:
- Zoning Sejak Menit 0: Gunakan mode Chaos (ungu) untuk menyerang Mage musuh setiap kali mereka mendekati minion. Damage awal yang besar akan memaksa mereka menjauh, membuat mereka tertinggal Gold dan EXP.

- Prioritas Wave: Setelah mencicil HP musuh, segera bersihkan minion. Dengan damage yang sakit, musuh akan ragu untuk melakukan kontes wave.

- Laning Sustain: Jika HP-mu berkurang saat melakukan zoning, segera ganti ke mode Light (kuning) dan gunakan Skill 1 pada minion atau hero lawan untuk memulihkan HP tanpa perlu melakukan recall.

- Flicker Surprise: Begitu mencapai level 4, lakukan kejutan dengan kombo Ultimate Chaos + Flicker ke arah Mage musuh yang sedang sekarat untuk mendapatkan kill pertama.
dominants.dominant_title: Early Game Dominance: Aggressive Zoning & Wave Denial

dominants.dominant_desc:
Tips & Tricks:

Eksekusi Lini Belakang (Backline Pick-off): Di fase ini, kamu harus mulai mencari peluang untuk melenyapkan Marksman atau Mage musuh. Gunakan kejutan Ultimate Chaos + Flicker untuk langsung masuk ke posisi mereka saat mereka merasa aman di belakang Tank.

Objektif Cepat (Turtle/Lord): Lunox adalah salah satu Mage tercepat dalam menghancurkan objektif besar. Selalu hadir saat kontes Turtle atau Lord dan gunakan mode Chaos (Ungu) secara maksimal untuk memberikan damage persentase HP yang sangat besar pada objektif tersebut.

Rotasi dan Zoning: Setelah membersihkan wave di Mid, jangan hanya diam. Lakukan rotasi ke Gold Lane atau area buff musuh untuk memberikan tekanan. Gunakan mode Chaos untuk mengusir musuh dari area penting (zoning) agar timmu bisa mengambil objektif dengan aman.

Sustain Tanpa Recall: Jika HP berkurang setelah war kecil, manfaatkan minion atau monster hutan untuk mengisi kembali darah menggunakan Skill 1 (Mode Light). Ini memungkinkanmu untuk terus memberikan tekanan tanpa harus kehilangan momentum karena sering melakukan recall.

Tips & Tricks:

Penghancur Tank Sejati: Di fase ini, pasif Lunox yang mengubah Cooldown Reduction menjadi Magic Penetration sudah mencapai titik maksimal. Jangan ragu untuk menyerang Tank lawan menggunakan mode Chaos (Ungu), karena pertahanan mereka akan terasa sangat tipis di hadapanmu.

Eksekusi High-Ground Defense: Jika musuh bermain bertahan di dalam base, gunakan kombo Ultimate Chaos + Flicker untuk melakukan serangan kejutan ke lini belakang mereka. Ledakan AOE dari Ultimate ungu dan tambahan Magic Power dari Holy Crystal dapat melenyapkan hero tipis lawan dalam sekejap.

Transisi Bertahan (Sustain): Setelah masuk ke tengah kerumunan musuh dengan mode ungu, segera tekan Skill 1 (Kuning) dan aktifkan Ultimate Brilliance (Cahaya). Ini akan memberimu status invincible (tidak bisa ditarget) sekaligus memberikan damage area tambahan sebagai penyelesaian (finishing).

Item Swapping (Strategi Bertahan): Di menit-menit krusial, kamu bisa menjual Sky Piercer atau Glowing Wand untuk membeli Winter Crown atau Immortality. Gunakan Winter Crown segera setelah durasi Ultimate kuningmu berakhir untuk memperpanjang waktu sustain di tengah pertempuran.

Fokus Target Utama: Selalu incar Marksman atau Mage lawan yang menjadi sumber damage utama tim mereka. Menghilangkan satu damage dealer musuh menggunakan kombo agresifmu akan memastikan timmu memenangkan war terakhir.
dominants.dominant_title: The Ultimate Shield Breaker & Backline Finisher

emblems.emblem_desc: Pemilihan set emblem ini didasarkan pada kebutuhan untuk menyeimbangkan dominasi di awal permainan dan kekuatan penghancur di akhir laga, yang sangat sesuai dengan teknik Flicker-Chaos yang kamu gunakan untuk mengincar lini belakang.

equips.equip_desc:
Why I Choose This Build:
Build ini dirancang khusus untuk memaksimalkan potensi snowball Lunox sejak menit awal. Fokus utamanya adalah Magic Penetration dan Execution Power. Dengan kombinasi item ini, Lunox tidak hanya menjadi Mage yang mencicil HP musuh, tetapi menjadi eksekutor mematikan yang mampu melenyapkan lini belakang musuh dalam sekejap melalui kombo Flicker + Chaos Ultimate.

Key Equipment Highlights:

Arcane Boots & Sky Piercer: Inilah mesin penggerak agresivitas di early game. Arcane Boots memberikan penetrasi instan, sementara Sky Piercer memastikan setiap target yang sekarat akibat kombo Chaos langsung tereliminasi (eksekusi), memungkinkan Lunox mendapatkan stack dan unggul gold lebih cepat.

Holy Crystal & Divine Glaive: Kombinasi maut untuk late game. Holy Crystal meningkatkan Magic Power secara masif, sedangkan Divine Glaive memastikan damage kita tetap menembus pertahanan Tank atau hero yang sudah membeli Magic Defense tebal.

Glowing Wand: Sangat krusial untuk memberikan efek burn tambahan (persentase HP) dan mengurangi efek regenerasi lawan. Ini sangat efektif untuk membantu finishing saat kita berganti ke mode Light Ultimate.

Blood Wings: Memberikan tambahan Magic Power tertinggi sekaligus Shield dan Movement Speed ekstra. Ini memberikan sedikit napas tambahan (sustain) agar Lunox tidak langsung mati saat melakukan dive ke tengah kerumunan musuh.

Situational Adjustments:

Early Game Alternative: Jika musuh tidak terlalu tebal, Genius Wand bisa digunakan sebelum Holy Crystal untuk mengurangi defense lawan secara agresif.

Late Game Defense: Jika permainan sangat intens dan berisiko, saya sering mengganti Glowing Wand atau Sky Piercer (jika stack rendah) dengan Winter Crown atau Immortality untuk memberikan peluang hidup kedua setelah melakukan inisiasi kombo ke lini belakang.

hero.hero_overview: Lunox adalah hero Mage dengan spesialisasi Burst/Magic Damage yang sangat bergantung pada penguasaan mekanik "Balance". Hero ini sangat unik karena memiliki dua mode tempur: Mode Light untuk bertahan (invincible) dan Mode Chaos untuk memberikan damage berkelanjutan secara instan. Sebagai Midlaner, Lunox difokuskan untuk memenangkan fase laning dengan agresif sejak level awal dan menjadi penghancur Tank di late game melalui Magic Penetration alaminya. Strategi ini sangat cocok untuk pemain yang menyukai analisis hero build dan eksekusi strategi pertandingan yang presisi.
hero.hero_strength:
Agresivitas Early Game: Memiliki damage poke yang sangat tinggi menggunakan Skill 2 (Chaos Assault) sehingga bisa menekan lawan sejak menit pertama.

Anti-Tank Sejati: Berkat pasifnya, Lunox bisa mengubah cooldown reduction menjadi Magic Penetration, menjadikannya mimpi buruk bagi hero dengan defense tebal di akhir laga.

Survivability Tinggi: Memiliki kemampuan untuk menjadi tidak bisa ditarget (invincible) melalui Ultimate mode Light, yang sangat efektif untuk menghindari serangan kejutan atau burst damage lawan.

Snowballing: Jika mendapatkan keunggulan gold di awal, Lunox sulit dihentikan karena kemampuannya melakukan instant kill pada hero tipis lawan.
hero.hero_weakness:
Manajemen Mana: Penggunaan skill yang agresif di awal permainan sering kali membuat Lunox sangat boros Mana, sehingga sangat bergantung pada item regenerasi atau Blue Buff.

Mekanik Stack yang Kompleks: Memerlukan ketepatan dalam mengatur stack (kuning/ungu); kesalahan posisi stack di tengah war bisa berakibat fatal bagi Lunox.

Jarak Serang Terbatas: Sebagai seorang Mage, Lunox memiliki jarak serang yang relatif pendek, menuntut pemain untuk memiliki positioning yang sangat akurat agar tidak mudah terkena crowd control.

Ketergantungan Level: Performa agresifnya sangat bergantung pada pencapaian Level 4 secepat mungkin untuk membuka potensi Ultimate-nya.

data.recommend: Bukan sekadar Mage, ini adalah mesin eksekusi! Bongkar teknik rahasia 'Flicker-Chaos Transition' yang bikin lini belakang musuh kena mental sebelum sempat bereaksi. Early game zoning, late game carry!

spell.spell_desc:
Why I Choose Flicker:
Flicker adalah Battle Spell wajib bagi Lunox Pure Mid karena memberikan mobilitas instan yang tidak dimiliki oleh skill set-nya secara alami. Spell ini sangat krusial untuk melakukan positioning ofensif guna mengincar backline (Mage/Marksman) musuh yang biasanya berada di posisi terlindungi. Selain itu, Flicker menjadi satu-satunya alat pertahanan instan untuk survive saat terdesak ketika Ultimate mode Light sedang dalam masa cooldown.

Advanced Combo: The Surprise Backline Diver
Kombo ini adalah kunci untuk gaya bermain agresif yang mampu menembus lini pertahanan musuh secara instan dan keluar dengan selamat.
Urutan Kombo:
1. Preparation: Pastikan kamu berada pada 1 Stack Ungu (Chaos).
2. The Initiation: Gunakan Ultimate Chaos + Flicker secara bersamaan ke arah target. Flicker akan memperjauh jarak terjang Ultimate ungu, sementara damage AOE dari ledakan awal Ultimate tersebut akan langsung mengenai kerumunan musuh di tengah atau belakang.
3. The Execution: Lakukan spam Skill 2 (Chaos Assault) beberapa kali untuk menghabisi target utama dengan Magic Penetration maksimal.
4. The Transition: Setelah target tumbang atau saat posisi mulai terdesak, gunakan Skill 1 (Starlight Pulse) dengan cepat untuk mengubah stack menjadi kuning.
5. The Finisher & Escape: Segera aktifkan Ultimate Brilliance (Kuning). Ini akan memberikan damage AOE tambahan sebagai penyelesaian (finishing), sekaligus memberikan efek immune (tidak bisa ditarget) agar kamu bisa mengatur ulang posisi atau keluar dari zona berbahaya dengan aman.
Mengapa Kombo Ini Sangat Mematikan?

Efek Kejutan Maksimal: Musuh tidak akan menyangka Lunox bisa menjangkau jarak sejauh itu dengan kombinasi Ultimate dan Flicker.

AOE Damage Ganda: Memanfaatkan ledakan awal dari mode Chaos dan diakhiri dengan ledakan dari mode Light.

Sustain yang Terjamin: Kamu tidak hanya masuk untuk mati (suicide mission), tapi memiliki rencana keluar yang pasti dengan status invincible dari Ultimate kuning.

hero.hero_overview: Lunox is a flexible Mage known for her dual Order/Chaos mechanics, offering high burst damage (Chaos) and invincibility/healing (Order), making her strong in 1v1s and survivable but vulnerable to CC if used poorly. Her skills shift between light and dark modes, allowing her to heal, deal AoE damage, blink, and reduce cooldowns. Mastering her involves balancing mana, managing the Order/Chaos meter, and timing her potent but skill-dependent ultimate, "Order & Chaos," for maximum impact.
hero.hero_strength: Lunox's strength comes from her dual forms (Order/Chaos), offering unmatched flexibility, high burst damage, and survivability through invincibility and healing, making her a strong carry capable of melting tanks (Chaos ult) and escaping or sustaining (Order ult). While hard to master due to high skill dependency and limited CC, mastering her passive, builds, and combos allows her to dominate lanes and teamfights by adapting to any situation, especially after recent buffs to her ultimate switching.
hero.hero_weakness: Lunox's main weaknesses are her short skill range, lack of hard crowd control (CC), and vulnerability during her Dark Ult, making her susceptible to heroes with high mobility, instant CC (stuns/suppression), or strong early-game pressure to prevent her scaling. Countering her involves baiting her skills, engaging when her CC is down, or using heroes with strong lockdown or invisibility to shut her down.

spell.spell_desc: For enhanced mobility to reposition, escape danger (like assassins), initiate surprise attacks, and execute powerful combos that blend her skills with the blink for unexpected burst damage, compensating for her lack of natural crowd control. It turns her from a vulnerable mage into a highly adaptable threat, letting her surprise squishies or escape CC before she's locked down.

Before and during team fights, Lunox players need to be mindful of several key precautions to maximize her effectiveness and avoid getting eliminated quickly:
 * Positioning is Paramount: Due to her relatively short skill range and vulnerability during her Darkening ultimate, Lunox needs to maintain excellent positioning. Stay behind the frontline, near or behind other damage dealers or supports, to avoid being the primary target of enemy assassins or fighters.
 * Awareness of Enemy Crowd Control (CC): Lunox is highly susceptible to hard CC. Track the cooldowns and locations of enemy heroes with stuns, roots, or suppresses. Avoid being caught out by these abilities, as they can interrupt her combos and leave her vulnerable.
 * Ultimate Timing: Both of Lunox's ultimates require careful timing.
   * Power of Chaos (Darkening): Avoid using this ultimate in the middle of a chaotic fight where you're likely to be interrupted. Look for isolated targets or use it when enemy CC abilities have been used. Be mindful of the channeling time, as you're stationary during it.
   * Power of Order (Brilliance): While it grants invulnerability, don't use it recklessly. Use it to dodge crucial enemy ultimates, survive burst damage, or reposition aggressively when needed. Be aware of its relatively short duration.
 * Mana Management: Even with Demon Shoes or mana-restoring items, be mindful of your mana consumption, especially in prolonged team fights. Avoid unnecessary skill spam.
 * Respecting Enemy Burst Damage: Be aware of enemy heroes who can deal significant burst damage quickly. Avoid being in their direct line of sight if your Power of Order is on cooldown.
 * Vision Control: Ensure your team has adequate vision of key areas to anticipate enemy engagements and avoid being ambushed. Knowing enemy positions allows you to position yourself more safely.

hero.hero_overview: Lunox, the Twilight Goddess in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, is a unique and powerful mage known for her ability to manipulate the powers of Order and Chaos.
hero.hero_strength:
Lunox is a unique mage who blends high damage potential with impressive survivability and mobility, making her a significant threat in the right hands.

She can also be immune to a lot of cc due tu her light ultimate which will be an advantage in case enemy heroes gank your lane. 
Here's some advantages of using her in gold lane:

* High Burst Damage: Her Chaos Assault (Darkening) can deal significant damage, especially to targets with high HP, making her effective against tanks and durable heroes. When enhanced by her ultimate, Power of Chaos, this burst potential becomes even more dangerous.
 * Sustain and Durability: Starlight Pulse (Brilliance) provides her with healing, especially when hitting enemy heroes. Her ultimate, Power of Order, grants her invulnerability while dealing continuous damage, allowing her to survive engagements and deal consistent damage.
 * Mobility and Untargetability: The Power of Order ultimate makes her untargetable and allows her to reposition, making her difficult to lock down and kill. Her Power of Chaos ultimate also provides a blink, enhancing her mobility and allowing for quick escapes or aggressive plays.
 * Lane Control and Harassment: Her skills allow her to effectively clear minion waves and poke enemies in lane, giving her good early to mid-game presence.
 * Versatility: Lunox can adapt her playstyle depending on the situation. She can focus on dealing burst damage with her Chaos form or provide sustained damage and survivability with her Order form.
 * Magic Penetration: Her passive ability converts a portion of her Cooldown Reduction into Magic Penetration when in Chaos form, allowing her to deal more damage to magic-resistant enemies.
 * Adaptable Builds: Lunox can utilize various item builds to enhance her damage, survivability, or mobility depending on the enemy composition and the flow of the game.
hero.hero_weakness:
Lunox, despite her strengths, also has notable weaknesses:
 * Short Range: Compared to many other mages like Cecilion, Xavier, or Novaria, Lunox's skill ranges are relatively short. This forces her to get closer to enemies, putting her at greater risk.
 * Vulnerable During Darkening Ultimate: While her Power of Chaos (Darkening) ultimate deals significant damage, she becomes stationary during its channeling. This makes her an easy target for crowd control (CC) abilities.
 * Reliance on Skill Combos: Lunox's effectiveness often depends on landing her skill combinations correctly. If her skills are interrupted or miss, her damage and survivability can be significantly reduced.
 * Susceptible to Hard CC: Like most mages, Lunox is vulnerable to hard crowd control effects like stuns, roots, and suppress. Heroes with reliable CC can easily disrupt her combos and lock her down.
 * Limited Mobility Without Ultimates: While her ultimates provide mobility, her basic skills lack strong movement options. If her ultimates are on cooldown, she can be easily caught out of position.
 * Can Be Shut Down by Magic Resistance: While her passive provides some magic penetration in Chaos form, building significant magic resistance against her can reduce her burst damage potential.
 * Teamfight Positioning is Crucial: Due to her short range and vulnerability during her Darkening ultimate, proper positioning in team fights is critical. If she's poorly positioned, she can be easily focused and eliminated.
 * Item Dependent: Like most mages, Lunox's power scales significantly with items. If she falls behind in farm, her impact on the game can be limited.
In summary, while Lunox offers a unique blend of damage and survivability, her short range, vulnerability to CC, and reliance on skill combos make her a high-risk, high-reward hero that requires careful execution and positioning.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Lunox's Chaos Assault comes from 3 factors:
- Base damage (Level up with skill)
- Magic Power (Depend on Lunox's item)
- Your max health

Among the three, you can control the last, and assumes that Lunox will handle the first two good. So you should prioritize on:

1. General Damage Reduction: Vengeance, Queen's Wings, Twilight, Passives
2. Shield Sources: Aegis, Rose Gold, Magic Blade, Bloodwings, abilities
3. Things that waste Lunox's ult duration: Immortality, Ice Crown, CC, ...
3. Items that gives both health and magic defense, so that Lunox cannot focus on one early: Thunderbelt, Radiant Armor

Avoid items that only give high health, like from talents.

Be ahead of Lunox's items. If she can only buy Genius Wand, go for items that give health or amplify your healing. If she can go for Glowing Wand, go for hardcore magic defense items. When she goes Divine Glaive, go for damage reduction and shield items.

End the game early, as Lunox can turn the tide when game reaches 18 minute with 5 items and a Divine Glaive among.

As a tank, you don't have much finance for items. Opt for items that have multiple building path 

Vitality Crystal can be upgrade to either a Thunderbelt, a Radiant Armor or Twilight Armor, considering Lunox's teammates.

2x Cloak + Vitality provides almost the same stats, and have more combinations than a Silence Robe.

Don't rush Silence Robe too early on, as Lunox can prioritize Divine Glaive as soon as 3rd item, after Genius Wand. You cannot withstand 3x penetration items at once. Silence Robe has very limited build path.

Avoid buying Cloak and Vitality Crystal together, as they will likely to prompt a Lunox to predict a possible Silence Robe.

Also buy some armor or items like Dominance Ice to validate other enemies, instead of focusing too much on Lunox.

If you have multiple CC sources, like Ruby, Khufra, Gatotcaka, it is easy to enter a combat against Lunox. Otherwise, save until Lunox uses Dark ultimate.

When Lunox uses Dark ultimate on a tank, you can endure 1 second of it, then you will trigger any item passive like Bloodwings, Rose Gold to survive another second. If you cannot stun Lunox for the rest, rely on constant running or Immortality or Winter Crown.

Keep in mind if you go for Athena shield, Lunox might bait your passive first.

This combo is very sicked. You go high health, which will trigger a lot of damage from Chaos Assault, which in turn triggers a lot of damage from Lantern. This damage is so devastating that even if you are under Athena shield's passive, Lunox can damage you as if nothing.

Be very mindful about your health items, consider swapping one or two when Lunox has both these items.

A bad Lunox is the one that does not max out her potential, which will make her counter even more effective:
- Purchasing Lightning Truncheon or Ice Queen Wand, whose damage can be easily negated by magic defense.
- Choosing Mage emblem, purchasing Enchanted Talisman or don't switch to Magic PEN boots late game, which cost a lot of Chaos Assault
- Don't buy Divine Glaive or Glowing Wand even when you have a decent magic defense item or high healing ability
- Don't buy core items like Genius Wand or Holy Crystal
- Shoot very few Chaos Assaults in an ultimate or use Light ultimate too frantically
- Don't buy Lantern when you have 2+ items that give around 1000 health

emblems.emblem_desc:
Firmness and Tenacity gives you 21 hybrid defense as a tank, even a Genius Wand can hardly bypass early on.

Brave Smite recovers health and has low cooldown.

equips.equip_desc:
Tough boots give you good slow duration reduction and magic defense as Lunox doesn't have any PEN item early on.

Don't save it at 8 minute mark if you have to bear the damage frequently.

Healing Necklace and Cloak can be upgrade to another item later on.

Silence Robe is a worse item to rush, do you know why? 

It costs more than 1000 gold, but 2x cloak +vitality crystal gives almost the stats at 72% price! It also prompts Lunox to rush Divine Glaive early.

You should have at least a Radiant Armor by mid game.

Don't rush Athena Shield, as Lunox will bait its passive with 2nd or 3rd skill first, while Radiant Armor can max stack as she finishes first half combo.

Don't buy any more Cloak this point, as Lunox will notice you buy all magic defense items and will go Divine Glaive.

A half health and half magic defense will make Lunox choose between Lantern or Divine Glaive first.

Twilight Armor is better than Athena shield, as it also blocks physical damage, and it reduces every Chaos Assault which surely deals more than 500 dmg per instance.

Rose Gold will make Lunox surprise when she thought she is about to slay you.

Winter Crown wastes the remaining time of Lunox's dark ultimate. But keep in mind its cooldown is 3 times of Lunox.

Queen's Wings also gives damage reduction and cooldown reduction for skills to counter. It has a short cooldown as Rose Gold that can be used in every combat.

Most Lunox cannot do anything if you respawn, but keep in mind to swap Immortality with other items.

1550 HP helps Lunox take you down in one second. Such amount only equals to 1.5x Chaos Assault without any magic defense.

Cursed Helmet gives you little magic defense, poor passive and a huge health that helps Lunox end you faster.

Oracle: Most Lunox will go for Wand to reduce healing, so the little health and heal amp bonus is nothing

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
Atlas gives good crowd control and prevents enemies from fleeing away for Lunox to spam Chaos Assault. Lunox ensures her damage output makes better use of Atlas' one or two cc early on than most other marksmen before lv4.

Though he is less tanky than Tigreal, his chasing speed makes marksmen with only one mobility skill or conceal hopeless.

Chip allows Lunox's continuous presence after she learned her ultimates, contribute to Turtle and Lord fight and come back to Gold Lane just in time.

Chip is not tanky and has mild control effect, yet he has decent roaming speed.
Hylos gives Lunox everything she needs: tankiness, high mobility to flee or pursue, a small stun and healing from Revitalize.

Khufra gives Lunox tankiness and crowd control from a safe distance. Enemy marksmen attempting to dash away afterwards will be canceled by his ball while those running towards bushes will be stunned. Khufra also provides slow, just in case Lunox missed once.

counters.counter_desc:
Grock makes Lunox players must beware of the wall behind her, as it will hamper her Dark ultimate' duration and she loses significant health if being hit by a Grock suddenly stepping out from bush.

Grock can heal up the damage taken and most importantly, the wall strategically blocks Lunox' Light ultimate's pathing.

When timed properly, Lolita' shield will send back all Lunox' Chaos Assault. Any of the slow and double stun force Lunox to use her Light ultimate, which makes her vulnerable right after with the remaining stun not used.

Lunox can easily counter Lesley. All Lunox's skills remove Lesley's concealment. What that is worse than a slowed Lesley is that Lunox can block all of her sniping bullets with proper distance during Light ultimate.

Lesley needs at least 3 enhanced basic attacks to take down Lunox while the other can heal back and slay her in less than 2 seconds. A proper Lunox users can Chaos Assault Lesley at a range further than her 2nd skill, which only briefly interrupts Lunox's Dark ultimate if succeeds.

Lunox does not really have a crowd control that needs removing by Wanwan. Wanwan's attack range falls within Lunox's and her attack speed surely cannot matches Lunox's casting abilities before hitting all weaknesses. And even if she does, a single Light ultimate outlasts Wanwan's ultimate duration.

dominants.dominant_desc:
If you go bottom lane, you should reach the bush near the Gold Grab, as the minions will meet far awar from the first turret.

If you are in the three continuous bushes, you can use first ability to safely check if enemy is in the next bush.

Use third ability to check bush from a safe distance. Aim for the area that the enemy most likely situates.

Always check enemy's item progress to adapt accordingly. They might buy an attack speed, attack damage or magic defense first.

Try to have 2 small items giving movement speed early to avoid enemy marksman's hit and run technique.

Never go to high on the first 2 minion waves. After the 2nd minion waves, stay under your turret until the first turtle as this is the most vulnerable time for Lunox: She has no ultimate and enemy jungler reaches lv4. With careful micro and sidestep control effects, no one can solo kill Lunox at level 4 under the turret when her team is away for turtle.

When the enemy is not in your lane, if you want to heal more, stay far away from the minions when use first skill and don't do any basic attacks. This way you can heal up to three times per minions.

When you want to clear faster before enemy returns or heal back quickly, stay close to minions and attack as much as possible.

You can tank minion's damage to zone out enemies, as you can easily heal back the lost amount. Keep in mind the healing amount diminishes as minions die.

Before attacking the turtle, use the first ability to attract the Gold Crab nearby. It will help you attract it again to heal or check nearby enemies in bush.

Use it when fleeing enemies like Natalia, Lesley or Miya conceals or invisible.

Try staying on high health to reduce chance of being ganked.

Always use first ability before Light ultimate, so that you will return with more health than enemy anticipated. You can pretend running towards the minions to make use of the heal.

Use second ability follows up with basic attack to poke.

Use second ability to secure last hit.

Keep in mind this ability is very mana consuming, so instead of spamming it as soon as it goes off cooldown, sync it with Impure Rage to max out poke damage or lessen mana cost.

Can also be used to check small bush and exposes conceal.

Only dive turret or use up all ultimates if being sure the position of other enemies on minimap. Try to use one ultimate only at a time, so that you still have an offensive of defensive option during the long cooldown at lv1.

Don't hesitate to use Dark ultimate to slay a Gold Crab or Lithowanderer, and don't let enemy know or make use of your ultimate on cooldown afterwards.

When multiple enemies are attempting to dive turrets or when solo 1v1, try to take some damage first to cause them to take damage before using your ultimate, forcing them to flee or take even more damage while you are healed a little.

Since side lanes have at least 4 bushes, anticipate enemies running into bush to waste your either ultimates or exploit the bush to safely land after Light ultimate.

Most marksman only has none to one dash with limited mobility, try to slow them with third ability and center them righ under your light ultimate to maximize damage.

Light ultimate is effective against ultimate from Wanwan, Lesley, Brody, Beatrix,...

Your Dark ultimate will greatly help contest Turtle if it is near your lane.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Assassin Emblem and Rupture eliminate all Magic PEN on squishy enemy marksman and other enemies later in late game. This build provides optimal between damage and magic PEN.

Weapon Mastery: Since she already has gold advantage in Gold Lane, all she needs is some damage to harass them or heal back up.

Impure Rage: Ease some mana issues, increase poking capability.

equips.equip_desc: Since she has gold advantage, items like Genius Wand and Holy Crystal can be bought early on, much better than Sky Piercer.

A single Demon Boots solves Lunox's mana problem.

Protip: buy Exotic Veil early as it gives you decent Magic Power and same speed as Sky Piercer to rotate Mid to farm or combat when needed. It can be either built into Genius Wand or Glowing Wand depending on situation.

If Lunox does not need spam abilities constantly, 2x Necklace will provide sufficient mana between each minion wave's break, and less cost investment early on.
This also gives decent magic PEN, meaning more damage to Gold Crab and enemy marksman.
equips.equip_title: Starting: Level 4: more damage and PEN

equips.equip_desc:
Enemies can't heal much early on, so you opt for the first core item: Genius Wand. 2 items give magic pen and 2x Exotic Veil in the kit gives Lunox decent movement speed.

Otherwise, replace Genius Wand with Glowing Wand.

Dreadnaught Armor: You can acquire this item under 1 minute. Useful if enemy is Clint, Fanny or high physical damage. Don't buy Wings yet as it is not her core items, less effective and you can keep this armor for several minutes.

Your ultimate is at max level, you have 2 core items and a healing reduction. At this point, Advance Combo will do a lot of damage.
Selling all mana necklace for better damage output.

Antique Cuirass: for a team that you don't have any tank, upgrade Dreadnaught armor further as it gives more survivability against physical enemies than Wings. Otherwise sell it for another magic item or follow the first build.

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
Lunox needs a roamer who can provide crowd control effect, tankiness and secondary damage dealer when she is away for jungle. Edith is the best answer as her passive will help her clear buff faster than Tigreal or Johnson with similar benefits. In teamfight, when both of them slow and damage, enemies must choose one to invest crowd control.

Lunox can also slow for Edith to leash out her damage for them to alternately deal massive damage in 12 seconds.

The downside is that both of them are magical, which forces enemies to build magic defenses.

Belerick provides both crowd control, tankiness and movement speed to check bush. Enemies attempting to attack Lunox will hit Belerick's Vengeance spell.

Both of them deal magical damage.

counters.counter_desc:
Nolan has too many dashes to evade Lunox's attack range. Unless Lunox pre-empts Nolan's ultimate with her light ultimate, she will be slain before taking down Nolan, during which Lunox must stand still to cast Chaos Assault.

No Flicker even worsens Lunox' mobility, while both players carry Ice Retribution will cancel each other out. Nolan's low cooldown will make his gank less costly than Lunox's.
Lunox's triple slows and Light ultimate make Harith unable to dash constantly and deter him away from his cross. Harith's little shield and low health will be melted down quickly

dominants.dominant_desc: You should get Purple Buff first, because Purple Buff has higher health than Red Buff, which makes it take more damage from your Chaos Assault, the duo can be cleared quickly with your first skill and by the time you get lv4, you will have more health and mana left than you go Red Buff route. Also, the Red Buff will last will help you slay Turtle faster. Ask your team to help you out as enemies are more likely to invade if seeing a Lunox jungler.

Try to slay Lithowanderer if possible, as it gives her mana and speed on the river. Always make use of river surge to compensate her slow speed. When jungling, always slay Buffs in between other creeps slay so that your ultimate has some time to 

When passing by mid lane, Lunox can clear wave quickly which also helps her restore some health.

Try to stay dead as little as possible, as you are the main damage output early on.

Avoid ganking if enemy comes in team, or you have no ally, or before Turtle fight, as her ultimates' cooldown is still moderately high (25+ seconds early game)

With Ice Retribution, combine it with basic or advance combo to slow enemies. Since the slow stacks additively, whether you cast Retri or slow from abilities first don't matter much.

If you can get kills, try to not stay behind enemy jungler' level so that you have equal retribution damage.

Ult on cooldown combo: 2-3-2
Basic combo: (Retri)-2-3-4-2(x10)
Advance combo (triple slow): 2-Retri-4(dark)-3-1-4-2-2-2

Lunox can use Retribution while being invincible during Light ultimate, use this to avoid CC, capture objectives and flee to safe place or pursue further. 4 seconds duration is long enough for you to decide.

In the first two turtle, a full Dark ultimate combo can do around 70-80% Turtle's health, so try to lower the turtle first, don't spam it right away if no allies are nearby, enemies are nearby.

At the last turtle, a single Dark ultimate combo at lv3 can slay a turtle.

Similar to Turtle Contest, make use of Light ultimate to Retri and can combine with dark ultimate to flee or engage in teamfight right afterwards.

Use 3rd skill on bushes to check enemies camping from a safe distance before contesting Turtle. Keep in mind that using Dark ultimate will make you vulnerable to Lord's airbone. If allies are nearby, let them tank Lord's damage and lightning.

Use Flame Retribution and Red Buff to make Chaos Assault even more deadly.
Since she can clear most jungle camps within seconds, rely on them to heal back after fight instead of teleporting back.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Compared to her other roles, Lunox does not have much Emblem option when jungling.

Assassin Emblem & Rupture: gives Penetration against Creeps, especially when she does not have any Magic PEN items early on and reduces risk on ganking.

Seasoned Hunter: Quick turtle and lord slay.

Impure Rage: Impure Rage heals Lunox and deals extra damage on creeps and lords, which Lethal Ignition and War Cry cannot. Before Lv4, she can trigger this 4 times on a Red or Purple Buff.

equips.equip_desc:
This build is exactly the same as her build as mid laner.

Dire Hit Blessing fully stacks with her 3rd and Dark ultimate, render enemies' Flicker or dash helpless when she is under Light ultimate and retreats from diving turret safely.

It also allows her to steal enemy's speed while fleeing with Light ultimate's bonus speed when being ganked.

It can also be used as a backup when 3rd skill is on cooldown or when in need of slowing multiple enemies.

Unlike in other lanes, since your mana and health is healed with Purple Buff so Demon Shoes or Enchanted Talisman is redundant.

If you are confident about slow from your ability, Flame Retribution means more damage and heal from your combo.

Blood Wings gives more damage, movement speed and survivability than Sky Piercer if you don't intend to secure kills.

hero.hero_overview:
How many mages can jungle? Well, Lunox is rare among them. People say her jungle era is long gone, but this guide will show you how it is possible.

Please have a look at my other guides if you play her in other lanes.
hero.hero_strength:
All abilities suitable for jungling. Low cooldown, high sustanence, moderate jungle speed, high lord and turtle slaying speed.

Buffs pair well with her kit. Suitable for buff stealing.
hero.hero_weakness: Slow movement speed, abilities dependent and high resource investment early games.

data.title: Lunox Jungle Guide for ALL RANKS!

cooperates.cooperate_desc:
Tigreal provides chain crowd control for 2 seconds of Light ultimate or 4 seconds of Dark ultimate and tankiness that Lunox really needs, as well as deterrance when fleeing towards Tigreal. A single knockback and airbone in late game is enough for Lunox to take down an enemy.

He can also block some damage for Lunox to dive turret a little longer.

Johnson' stun, high mobility and low cooldown suits Lunox' kit well on team fight or lone ganking. Both of them are very difficult to take down if the enemies don't invest their entire kit on one.

When Lunox flees with Light ultimate, she can hop in Johnson rightaway.

counters.counter_desc: Terizla only has a knockback as crowd control, which does not affect Lunox' Dark ultimate much. His sluggishness pairs well with Lunox' multiple slow and Lunox can uses her light ultimate or just 1st skill to counter Terizla's delay damage. The more health Terizla has, the more damage she dealt, counters his damage reduction.
Gusion must personally dashes towards Lunox, and his animation can prompt Lunox to use her Light ultimate. Afterwards, she can easily defeat squishy Gusion who has no mobility skill left.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Core Lunox items are Holy Crystal, Genius Wand, rush them as soon as possible.

What to buy?
Remember to be flexible. If the game seems comfortable, you can opt for Lantern against enemies building HP items, Blood Wings for speed and survivability. Concentrated Energy gives her both lifesteal, health and magic power, but is only effective when you have her core items. Winter Truncheon for makeplay to survive after Light ultimate is worth considering.

What to avoid?
Do not buy Lightning Truncheon for her as she does not benefit much from Cooldown reduction and it gives total combo damage less than Genius Wand. Enchanted Talisman gives less Magic Power and wasteful cooldown reduction. If you prefer constant fighting, you can go for Fleeting Time to be more effective. Ice Queen Wand gives her everything she needs, but with little amount and she already has two slows, which makes it not recommended.
dominants.dominant_title: Items: What to buy and what to avoid?

dominants.dominant_desc:
Turn on Close Pursuit and Lock Hero to ensure you hit enemies during crucial Dark ultimate time

Use Manual Selection to slay minions first when defending turret against multiple enemy heroes.

You are only not harmed during Light ultimate but does not remove any control effect or bypass terrain. So beware even light control effects like Immobilize from Miya, Hanabi and Xavier or an ally Grock's Wall can stop you. The remaining duration of Light ultimate is visible to enemies.

Always direct you Dark ultimate: dash away to deter enemies, towards them to stack slows, or to the side to avoid being hit.

Your first and 2nd ability have the same range as your attack range. So gauge to not be too engaged or too remote. Max out the first 2 abilities first, as you need them at lv2 when you have ultimate. Also keep in mind that there's a delay before the waves return to you and will not return if the enemy dies.

Except for Zhask, you can play somehow aggressively with 1st skill to heal and clear wave. Reach lv2 means you have 3 abilities. Try to reach lv4 fast as both ultimates give you high offense and survivability.

From Level 4-8, only dive Turret when enemy is less than 25% health. Always in light mode to anticipate ganks, alway in dark mode if you use advance combo. Don't waste light ultimate quickly, let enemies like Odette, Joy, Jawhead, Aamon, Gusion use their ultimate first, and you can evade them with your Light ultimate!

From Level 12 after Holy Crystal, you can use Advance Combo. The double slow makes enemies helpless even with 1 Flicker or dash skill, and can dive turret and base.

Since your Dark ultimate only has 20s+ cooldown in late game, don't hesitate to use it to help Lord, Turtle or flee if you don't have combat in the very next moment. Keep in mind that 4s Dark ultimate is your main offensive time, so beware of enemy's crowd control.

Your 3rd skill can trigger Impure Rage or War Cry on enemies in bush from a safe distance. If you are close to a bush, your 1st skill can check the entire bush without having any animations revealed if there's no one.

You can also use 2nd skill to check bush, but it only works if you hits enemy's exact position, use on very small bush.

Do you know that some skins give Lunox some advantages than others? Dawn Revelation and Libra have very gentle Chaos Assault sound, Light ultimate animation and are hard to notice in a brawl fight until their tanks are melted! Use it if you believe the game is going to be hard.

Poking: 2-3-2
Clear wave: 1-3-1
Basic Dark combo: 2-3-4-2(x10 times)
Basic Light escape: 1-3 (to slow enemies)-4
Advance combo to dive turret: 4(dark)-(Flicker)-1-3-4-2-2-2
Advance combo to dive base: 4(dark)-Flicker-1-4
Counter gank: 3-4(light)-2-4-2(x10)

Always look out for enemy markman and mage, as in early to mid game you can always take them down 1v1 with just 4-5 Chaos Assault from dark combo. The next are Assassins, Supports and Fighters.

If possible, steal enemy's purple buff or asks your jungle in late game with a single Dark ultimate. The healing and cooldown reduction is worthy with her build focusing on damage and magic penetration.

If enemies successfully flee from your Light ultimate, always look out for a landing point as you'll become very vulnerable.

Be mindful when enemies fleeing towards a nearby bush. As it will make you waste 1-2 seconds of Dark ultimate, and get hit by their crowd control effects.

Remember that you can use any of your abilities to counter enemies with conceal like Lesley, Natalia or from Roaming Equipment. But most effective are your first and 3rd skill, which also slow enemies, so reserve these abilities in anticipation of enemy fleeing.

After each abilities cast, Lunox performs a basic attack almost instead, so remember to make use of it during laning phase to maximize Poking potential or trigger Sky Piercer effect.

You can add 1st skill in all of her ultimate combo to add a slight heal when desperately needed, as the 2nd skill will be reset instantly. With Holy Crystal and Concentrated Energy, she can heal herself to max health after performing Advance Combo.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Assassin set is better than Mage set, as it givesmore Magic PEN, compensate adaptive attack of Thrill that she did not choose and her slow movement speed to rotate. Cooldown reduction is very redundant in late game.

Rupture: before she gets Magic Pen items, this helps Lunox melts tank quickly when she is getting Magic Power items.

Weapon Master: massive damage dealt means massive lifesteal for her in late game, much better than Festival Blood when she already has Magic Pen items.

Impure Rage: Early game, it increases Lunox's poke ability, provides mana when clearing wave and has low cooldown the same as her 1st and 2nd abilities, which also deals damage based on max health against Tanky enemies. During Dark ultimate, she can activate this twice. Also can pair with 3rd skill to check bush from a safe distance or damage during Light ultimate. This also helps melting down Lord and Turtle.
emblems.emblem_title: Optimal damage, mana and poking playstyle

emblems.emblem_desc: The same as the first Emblem, but if your team also has another member with considerable damage output and prefer snowball playstyle, this item wil help you rush Holy Crystal and Genius Wand 30 seconds earlier.
This build helps you with better sustanence early game and support late game. If you manage to secure kills early on, the spell vamp bonus will turn into Magic PEN in late game.

This build maximizes your damage output and suitable for solo kill playstyle. But keep in mind you will constantly run into mana problem and War Cry cooldown makes you rely closely on both ultimates, with less heal and teamfight effeciveness.

equips.equip_desc:
Arcane Boots: Eliminate enemy's early magic defense. Remember to buy 2x Necklace first, or only replace Demon Shoes with it late game as she will surely run out of mana soon.

Sky Piercer: If you are sure to secure kills early on, it gives her moderate damage and speed she needs early.

Glowing Wand: Provides both speed to rotate, prevent tanks from healing or shielding and pairs with 2nd and 3rd skill to clear wave in an instant.

Holy Crystal: Lunox's core item. More magic power means chaos assault means more damage and 1st skill more heal. You must get it as soon as possible, and you can perform advance combo afterwards.

Genius Wand: Lunox's core item. Give decent Magic Power, Magic PEN early game and movement speed. You can build this instead of Sky Piercer if you feels inconfident.

Divine Glaive: Lunox's passive greatly gives her healing and Magic Pen from this item.
Against enemies with both high magic defense and health build. This build has 4 magic pen items: Arcane Boots, Genius Wand, Concentrated Energy, Divine Glaive and your passive!

This built gives you all mana you need in early game and should only be used if your team can end game quickly before enemies buy much magic defense.

Fleeting Time and 2 kills give you only few seconds without Light and Dark ultimate!

Blood Wings, Winter Crown gives even more survivability

hero.hero_overview: The most difficult mage in game! The probably most powerful in lore! The only mage with abilities to clear wave, tank melter, turret diver and healing all in one will be mastered in this guide.
hero.hero_strength:
Early game: Two skills at lv1, regen and clear lane at the same time, tank melter, quick lord and turtle eater, good bush checker.

Late game: turret diver (possibly base diver), extreme slow on enemies, high survivability against many assassins, low ability cooldown.
hero.hero_weakness:
Early: Very mana consuming, slow movement speed, melee engaging range.

Mid-late game: No hard cc. Best pair with teams that have defense and cc but lack damage against enemies with many tanks. Squishy for slow hand players or poor rotation. Sustanence is minimal late game.

Overall: Ping dependent (<10ms) to maximize Dark ultimate.

spell.spell_desc:

Throughout the game, 1 dash and 1 invincible from ultimate, plus Flicker will make Lunox extremely difficult to take down.
